q: What maintains the resting potential of a neuron at approximately -70 mV?
options: Equal concentrations of Na+ and K+ on both sides of the membrane
The sodium-potassium pump actively transports 3 Na+ out and 2 K+ in, maintaining a net negative interior
Voltage-gated calcium channels keep the membrane polarized
Myelin sheath prevents any ion movement across the axon membrane
explanation: The Na+/K+ ATPase continuously moves 3 Na+ out and 2 K+ in per cycle, maintaining the electrochemical gradient and net negative charge inside the neuron.
